sets.name: Talent Tracker
sets.description: 25-card NCAA prospect insert. AJ Dybantsa at TT-1 anchors the rainbow.
sets.editorialNote: The premier prospect insert. Same 25-player lineup as Gen Next, VIP, and Bowman Verified — Topps chose to spread the 2026 class across four identically-odds inserts rather than one deep set. Talent Tracker is the flagship design if you have to pick one.

sets.name: Gen Next
sets.description: 25-card NCAA prospect insert — same player pool as Talent Tracker with a different design.
sets.editorialNote: One of four structurally identical prospect inserts. Design differentiates but the checklist doesn't.

sets.name: Very Important Prospects
sets.description: 25-card NCAA prospect insert. Same 25 players as TT/GN/BV.
sets.editorialNote: Third of the four identical-odds prospect inserts. Checklist redundant with Talent Tracker.

sets.name: Bowman Verified
sets.description: 25-card NCAA prospect insert with verification-themed design.
sets.editorialNote: Fourth in the TT/GN/VIP/BV quartet. Same players, different wrapper.

sets.name: ROY Favorites
sets.description: 15-card NBA rookie insert highlighting Rookie of the Year candidates.
sets.editorialNote: One of Hobby's easier pulls (~1 per box). Flagg, Harper, and the top NBA picks are here — but so are Edgecombe, Johnson III, and Fears, three of the rookies who lack auto access in the product. For no-auto rookie exposure, ROY Favorites is the path.

sets.name: Hobby Stars
sets.description: 15-card established NBA star insert. Paolo Banchero, Steph Curry, Cooper Flagg.
sets.editorialNote: NBA star showcase at accessible pull rates. More veteran-focused than ROY Favorites.

sets.name: Young Kings
sets.description: 25-card insert split between current NBA stars and retired Hall of Famers in their original team colors.
sets.editorialNote: The sleeper insert of the product. YK-1 through YK-15 feature current stars (SGA, Luka, Wemby, LeBron), but YK-16 through YK-25 bring back NBA legends in their original uniforms: Iverson as a 76er, Magic as a Laker, Larry Bird as a Celtic, KG as a Timberwolf, and Ray Allen as a Seattle Supersonic — a team that hasn't existed since 2008. This is a rookies-vs-legends retrospective dressed as a standard insert. Shaq appears here (Lakers) separate from his Retrofractor LSU cards.

sets.name: Rockstar Rookies
sets.description: 20-card 2025 NBA rookie class insert.
sets.editorialNote: The rookie-only counterpart to Young Kings. Flagg, Harper, Edgecombe, Johnson III all here — no auto access but solid rainbow potential.

sets.name: Greatness Loading
sets.description: 25-card rookie/up-and-comer insert.
sets.editorialNote: Shares odds with Young Kings. Rookie-focused version of the same pull tier.

sets.name: Mega Rookies
sets.description: 25-card NBA rookie insert — Mega-exclusive with Mojo parallel rainbow.
sets.editorialNote: Mega buyers' rookie insert chase. The Mojo parallel structure mirrors the base-card Mojo family — Blue /150 through Rose Gold Mojo /1 — all Mega-only.

sets.name: Mega Prospects
sets.description: 25-card NCAA prospect insert — Mega-exclusive, NCAA equivalent of Mega Rookies.
sets.editorialNote: Dybantsa, Peterson, Watkins, and the rest of the TT/GN/VIP/BV pool show up here too — but with the Mojo rainbow instead of Refractors.

sets.name: Base Etched In Glass Variation
sets.description: Etched-glass parallel variation of top NBA base cards. Refractor-style with glass-effect design overlay.
sets.editorialNote: Case-hit tier. The Etched In Glass treatment has moved to basketball from its Baseball Bowman roots. Refractor /Unnumbered base, with Gold/Orange/Red/SuperFractor variants.

sets.name: Chrome Prospect Etched In Glass Variation
sets.description: NCAA prospect counterpart to Base Etched In Glass — top 2026 names on glass-effect chrome.
sets.editorialNote: Prospect-side Etched In Glass. Dybantsa, Peterson, top names only.

sets.name: Retrofractor Variation
sets.description: Legend-tier throwback using college photography. Bill Russell (San Francisco) and Shaquille O'Neal (LSU).
sets.editorialNote: Two legends, two cards — the hidden gem insert of the product. Bill Russell's college photo is an absolute rarity given his era. Shaq in LSU purple is the other side of the pairing. The auto variation adds Shaq's signature.

sets.name: Bowman Spotlights NBA
sets.description: Dark-background spotlight treatment on top NBA subjects.
sets.editorialNote: Carries over from Bowman Baseball. Flagg, Harper, Wemby, LeBron anchor the 10-card NBA run.

sets.name: Bowman Spotlights NIL
sets.description: NCAA/NIL counterpart to Spotlights NBA. Prospect spotlight treatment.
sets.editorialNote: 5-card NIL version — Dybantsa, Peterson, Watkins, top women. Tighter pull than NBA side.

sets.name: Crystalized NBA
sets.description: Geode/crystal-backed case hit. Debuted in 2025 Bowman Draft, now in basketball.
sets.editorialNote: The crystal-backed design is a visual standout. 10-card NBA run with Gold/Orange/Red Crystal Refractors and a SuperFractor chase.

sets.name: Crystalized NIL
sets.description: NCAA/NIL version of Crystalized. 5-card prospect-focused run.
sets.editorialNote: The NIL Crystalized cards feature top 2026 prospects. Juju Watkins + Stephen Curry comps from the Slabsquatch analysis suggest strong early secondary demand.

sets.name: Anime NBA
sets.description: Japanese anime-style illustrations of NBA stars. Topps' biggest case-hit hype carryover from baseball.
sets.editorialNote: The most-hyped case hit. Anime has outperformed secondary market expectations in Baseball and basketball demand is projected to eclipse it. 25-card run including Wemby, Giannis, Steph, Tatum, plus the top 7 rookies and top 2026 prospects.

sets.name: Anime NIL
sets.description: NCAA/NIL counterpart to Anime NBA. 5-card NCAA run.
sets.editorialNote: NIL Anime features Dybantsa, Peterson, Watkins + top 2026 names. Tighter pull rate than NBA side.

sets.name: Bowman GPK NBA
sets.description: Garbage Pail Kids art-style case hit. Topps' GPK IP meets Bowman Basketball.
sets.editorialNote: The wildcard of the product. GPK meets NBA in a playful, satirical illustration series. 5-card NBA run with Cooper Flagg as 'Anthony Ant' being a notable viral-potential chase.

sets.name: Bowman GPK NIL
sets.description: NCAA/NIL GPK variant. 4-card NIL run.
sets.editorialNote: Sienna Betts, Sarah Strong, AJ Dybantsa reimagined as GPK characters. Tightest pull of all the NIL case hits.

editorial.overview: 22 insert sets — 11 case hits, 14 new designs to Bowman Basketball. Four structurally identical prospect inserts (Talent Tracker, Gen Next, Very Important Prospects, Bowman Verified) spread the same ~25-player NCAA pool across different designs; Talent Tracker is the flagship. Mega-exclusive Mega Rookies and Mega Prospects bring Mojo parallels to the insert layer. Young Kings is the sleeper — a rookies-vs-legends retrospective with Allen Iverson, Larry Bird, Magic Johnson, and Ray Allen in Supersonics green. The case-hit tier is where the product earns its chase reputation: Anime (NBA + NIL), Crystalized (NBA + NIL), Bowman Spotlights (NBA + NIL), Retrofractor (Bill Russell + Shaq legends), GPK (NBA + NIL), and Etched In Glass. Six case-hit IP brands, each with their own mini-rainbow. One notable dynamic: VJ Edgecombe, Tre Johnson III, and Jeremiah Fears — the three rookies absent from all autograph sets — have 7-9 insert appearances each, making inserts the only Bowman chase path for their cards.
